codeReview.summary: Clean, well-structured implementation that covers all feature requirements. Docker Compose creates PostgreSQL 15 with correct credentials (totus/totus/totus on port 5432) and healthcheck. drizzle.config.ts is properly configured pointing to DATABASE_URL with schema path. src/db/index.ts exports a pg Pool (max 20 connections) and drizzle() wrapper with good error handling for missing DATABASE_URL. package.json has all three drizzle-kit scripts (db:generate, db:push, db:studio). .env.example updated with default DATABASE_URL. vitest.config.ts correctly configured to load .env.local via Vite's loadEnv. Four integration tests verify pool connectivity, correct database name, drizzle export, and drizzle query execution. All verification commands passed (docker compose up, pg_isready, typecheck, lint, test).

issues.description: No graceful shutdown hook for the connection pool. pool.end() is never called on process exit. While this is standard practice for Next.js apps (the process manages its own lifecycle), it can cause connection leaks during hot module replacement in dev mode. Tests properly clean up via afterAll, so this only affects the app runtime.

loadEnv(mode, process.cwd(), '') with empty prefix loads ALL environment variables from .env files into the test process, not just VITE_-prefixed ones. This is intentionally needed for DATABASE_URL but means all env vars (including secrets like ENCRYPTION_KEY, MOCK_AUTH_SECRET) are exposed to the test process. Acceptable for local dev but worth noting for future security considerations.

Uses non-null assertion (process.env.DATABASE_URL!) which would cause a runtime error with an unhelpful message if DATABASE_URL is not set. Unlike src/db/index.ts which has a descriptive error message, drizzle.config.ts relies on TypeScript's non-null assertion. Acceptable since drizzle-kit is CLI-only and the error would be obvious in context.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker discovered that Vitest does not automatically load .env.local files and had to configure loadEnv from 'vite' package to make DATABASE_URL available in tests. This is a useful pattern that all future workers writing integration tests will need.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows worker initially got test failures because DATABASE_URL was undefined, then tried two approaches: first importing loadEnv from vitest/config (failed), then successfully using loadEnv from 'vite'. Final vitest.config.ts uses: const env = loadEnv(mode, process.cwd(), ''); with env passed to test config.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The connection.test.ts uses dynamic import (await import('@/db')) inside beforeAll to handle the module-level DATABASE_URL check in src/db/index.ts. This is a pattern future database-related tests will need to follow since src/db/index.ts throws at module-load time if DATABASE_URL is missing.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/db/__tests__/connection.test.ts:8-11 — dynamic import pattern; src/db/index.ts:4-8 — module-level DATABASE_URL validation that throws.

summary: Feature scaffold-database-infra passes review. All requirements from the feature description are met: Docker Compose with PostgreSQL 15 (correct credentials/port), drizzle.config.ts pointing to DATABASE_URL, src/db/index.ts with Pool and drizzle() wrapper, schema placeholder, package.json scripts (db:generate, db:push, db:studio), updated .env.example, and 4 integration tests. Three non-blocking issues noted: missing pool shutdown hook, broad env var loading in vitest config, and non-null assertion in drizzle config. Worker followed scaffold-worker procedure, all verification commands passed cleanly.
